description: Install Miniforge in the development container.
name: Miniforge Installation

rc_files.description: Paths to shell configuration files to append conda initialization to.

activate_env.description:
Name of a conda environment to activate.
Adds `conda activate <env>` to the shell configuration files
specified in `rc_files`.
Only takes effect when `rc_files` is set.

conda_dir.description:
Path to the conda installation directory.
This is the directory where conda will be installed.
It corresponds to the `CONDA_DIR` environment variable.
If you use a custom path, add a matching `containerEnv`/`remoteEnv` entry
to your `devcontainer.json` — dynamic option references in `containerEnv`
are not yet supported by the devcontainer spec.

group.description:
Name of a user group to give access to conda.
Only applies when `set_permissions` is true.

install.description:
Install conda and mamba using the Miniforge installer.
Raises an error if conda is already installed.

installer_dir.description:
Path to a directory to download the installer to.
This is the directory where the Miniforge installer will be downloaded.

interactive.description:
Run the installer in interactive mode.
This allows the installer to prompt the user.
The default is to run the installer in non-interactive mode.

keep_installer.description: Keep the Miniforge installer script and checksum after installation instead of removing them.

logfile.description: Log all output (stdout + stderr) to this file in addition to console.

miniforge_name.description: Name of the Miniforge variant to install.

conda_version.description:
Version of conda to install (e.g. '24.7.1').
Defaults to 'latest'. Passed as-is to the Miniforge release URL.
Version resolution logic will be added in a later iteration.

reinstall.description:
Reinstall conda and mamba using the Miniforge installer.
Same as 'install', but uninstall first if conda is already installed.

set_permissions.description:
Set permissions for the conda installation directory.
Creates the conda group, adds all users listed in `users` to it,
and applies group ownership and group-write bits to the conda directory.

update_base.description:
Update the base conda environment.
This is done by running `conda update --all`.
This is not recommended for production environments.

update_path.description: Write /etc/profile.d/conda_path.sh so conda is available in subsequent shell sessions and Dockerfile RUN layers.

users.description:
Comma-separated list of users to add to the conda group (e.g. 'alice,bob').
Each user must already exist.
Only applies when `set_permissions` is true.
If empty, defaults to the user running the script.

require_root.description:
Whether root is required to run this script.
'auto' infers from conda_dir: system paths (/opt, /usr, /var, /srv, /snap) require root, user paths do not.
'true' always enforces root. 'false' always skips the check.
